rationale: The citing paper explicitly engages Roehm and Tybout (2006) in its theory discussion on scandal spillover, summarizing that spillover depends on category typicality and attribute diagnosticity. It then positions its own contribution as building on and extending that stream by examining transference when failures are attributed to algorithms versus humans, with a different mechanism (group homogeneity/social categorization). The citation is also tied to the paper's ancillary empirical comparison (brand-to-brand vs algorithm/person transference), so it plays a meaningful framing and interpretive role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Roehm and Tybout's scandal spillover line of work to AI-vs-human failure contexts. It uses the prior brand spillover account as a conceptual benchmark and then adds a new mechanism and setting to explain broader transference patterns.
